---
title: "Session 3: Modelling Binary and Categorical Outcomes"
date: "2026-05-07"
date-modified: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Date:** Thursday, 7 May 2026, 16:00–19:00
:::

## Business question

*Can we predict which firms are likely to exit the market in the next year?*

## Learning goals

- Understand when and why OLS breaks down for binary outcomes
- Run and interpret logistic regression with `glm()`
- Translate log-odds into meaningful business insights (predicted probabilities, marginal effects)

## Dataset

**bisnode-firms** — ~17,500 Hungarian firms in manufacturing and services (Békés & Kézdi 2021). Key variables: firm exit (binary), log sales, log employees, profit margin, firm age, liquidity, ownership structure.

## Session outline

- Debrief: Take-home Task 2
- Input: why OLS fails for binary outcomes; logistic regression intuition |
- Break 
- Live demo
- In-session exercise
- Debrief + Quarto skill: inline R code

## Materials

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| [Slides](https://data-science-ma.github.io/AdvancedDataScience26-Material/sessions/session_03/slides.html) | Lecture slides — open in browser, press `F` for fullscreen |
| [Lecture notes & demo](demo.qmd) | Live coding document built during the session |
| [Exercise sheet](exercise.qmd) | In-session exercise — predicting firm exit with logistic regression |
| [Exercise solution]() | An example solution for the exercise (added after session) |
